verdict: The chart features a fantastic representation of the song's syncopated Tresillo rhythm, but is held back by technical mapping errors and frustratingly tight jump-to-tap transitions.

issues.problem: A HOLD D is active during a JUMP LR at 13.203. This requires three panels to be pressed simultaneously on a single-dance chart, creating an impossible or highly uncomfortable physical conflict.
A 16th-note transition directly out of a JUMP LR into a tap D (86ms gap) at 173 BPM is extremely jarring to execute and highly prone to pad-misses or double-stepping.
Another extremely tight 16th-note transition immediately following a JUMP LR to a tap D, disrupting the flow of the song's drop.
A JUMP LR is placed exactly a 16th-note after tap U at 38.045, making the entry into the jump feel rushed and physically awkward at this tempo.
